front: Describe the gamma-carboxylation of glutamate and its biological importance.
back: Gamma-carboxylation of Glu to Gla (gamma-carboxyglutamate) is catalyzed by vitamin K-dependent carboxylase. Gla residues chelate Ca2+ ions and are essential for the function of clotting factors II, VII, IX, X and anticoagulant proteins C, S, Z.
